description:
You are acting as a release-manager assistant.  
Today’s release date is 2023-10-15 and some preliminary artefacts & metadata have already been prepared for you under your home directory.

Initial filesystem layout  
/home/user  
├── artifacts  
│   ├── app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz  
│   ├── app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz  
│   └── app3_v3.4.5.tar.gz    ← NOT part of today’s release  
└── releases  
    └── release_2023-10-15.csv  

File: /home/user/releases/release_2023-10-15.csv  
Contents (3 lines, Unix LF line-endings):  
app,version,artifact_file  
app1,1.2.3,/home/user/artifacts/app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz  
app2,0.9.8,/home/user/artifacts/app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz  

Task you must perform (3–4 normal shell commands is enough; no root access required):

1. Create a new deployment folder exactly at  
   /home/user/deployments/2023-10-15/  
   (create all parent directories that do not yet exist).

2. Copy **only** the artefacts listed in release_2023-10-15.csv into that
   new directory.  Do not copy any other files (for example app3_v3.4.5.tar.gz).

3. Inside /home/user/deployments/2023-10-15/ generate a plain-text file
   named deployment_summary.log whose contents follow this exact format:  
   • One summary line per artefact, ordered **alphabetically by application
     name (app column in CSV)**.  
   • Each summary line must look like  
       APP=<app_name> VERSION=<version> FILE=<basename> SIZE=<bytes>  
     where  
       <app_name>  – text from the “app” column  
       <version>   – text from the “version” column  
       <basename>  – file name only (e.g. app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz)  
       <bytes>     – exact file size in bytes of the copied file found
                      **in the deployment folder**, not the original location  
   • After the last artefact line, add a final line  
       TOTAL_ARTIFACTS=<N>  
     where <N> is the number of artefacts actually copied (for today it should
     be “2”).  

   Example of the required layout (values will be different if file sizes
   change, but the structure must be identical):  
   APP=foo VERSION=0.1.0 FILE=foo_0.1.0.tar.gz SIZE=123  
   APP=bar VERSION=9.9.9 FILE=bar_9.9.9.tar.gz SIZE=456  
   TOTAL_ARTIFACTS=2  

Hints (do NOT reveal exact commands):
• Simple tools such as “cut/awk”, “cp”, “wc ‑c”, “sort” are sufficient.
• Make sure the log file ends with a trailing newline so automated checks pass.
• Keep your working directory paths exactly as specified above.
truth:
Pre-existing artefact files and their exact contents (all end with a single LF):

1. /home/user/artifacts/app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz  
   Text content:  
   DUMMY CONTENT FOR app1 version 1.2.3\n  

2. /home/user/artifacts/app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz  
   Text content:  
   DUMMY CONTENT FOR app2 version 0.9.8\n  

3. /home/user/artifacts/app3_v3.4.5.tar.gz  
   Text content:  
   DUMMY CONTENT FOR app3 version 3.4.5\n  

Byte sizes (including the trailing LF) used for verification:  
• app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz  – 37 bytes  
• app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz  – 37 bytes  
• app3_v3.4.5.tar.gz  – 37 bytes  

Expected directory to exist **after** the agent completes:  
/home/user/deployments/2023-10-15/

Files that must exist *inside* that directory:  
1. app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz                (exact copy, 37 bytes)  
2. app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz                (exact copy, 37 bytes)  
3. deployment_summary.log            (see below)

Exact required contents of deployment_summary.log  
(APP lines alphabetically sorted, followed by final total line, each ending with LF):

APP=app1 VERSION=1.2.3 FILE=app1_v1.2.3.tar.gz SIZE=37  
APP=app2 VERSION=0.9.8 FILE=app2_v0.9.8.tar.gz SIZE=37  
TOTAL_ARTIFACTS=2\n  

(The automated checker will verify that 1) only the two correct artefacts were
copied, 2) their byte-for-byte contents match the originals, 3) the summary
file contains exactly the three lines above, including the trailing newline.)
